introduction: An infection of the meninges with the presence of pus and inflammatory cells in the cerebrospinal fluid. A medical emergency, associated with considerable morbidity and mortality. May be bacterial (Pneumococcus, Meningococcus, tubercle bacilli, Haemophilus influenzae), viral, fungal, protozoal, neoplastic or chemical. Epidemics occur in the Savannah region and are usually caused by Neisseria meningitidis.

features: Neck stiffness and positive Kernig's sign
Photophobia with alteration in level of consciousness
If due to Pneumococcal and H. influenzae infection, additional features are jaundice, pneumonia and heart failure
Meningococcal infection presents additionally with joint pain, joint swelling, red eyes and skin rash

Tuberculous infection presents with weight loss, cough with blood in sputum while those with underlying immunosuppression (HIV infection) may manifest severe weight loss, diarrhoea, mouth lesions and skin rash
Mass lesion with focal neurological deficits (tuberculoma)
Acute psychosis (Organic Brain Syndrome) and dementia (chronic meningitis and pachymeningitis due to syphilis)

non_drug: Attention to calories and fluid/electrolyte balance
Nursing care (e.g., frequent turning and bladder care) to prevent decubitus ulcers and urinary tract infection
drug: Initial therapy will depend on the age of the patient and the causative agent, hence should be guided by microbiology results.
Vancomycin for suspected Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infection (MRSA)
Intravenous penicillin G with caution due to possible severe anaphylactic reaction
Mannitol 20% solution for acute cerebral decompression
Rifampicin for chemoprophylaxis during meningococcal epidemics
Ciprofloxacin for chemoprophylaxis during meningococcal epidemics
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Diazepam: Must be administered slowly intravenously to avoid respiratory depression
Mannitol extravasation causes inflammation and thrombophlebitis. Contraindicated in congestive cardiac failure and pulmonary oedema

complications: Cranial nerve palsies notably blindness and deafness
Syndrome of Inappropriate Anti-Diuretic Hormone secretion (SIADH)
prevention: Immunization of close contacts against communicable diseases - Meningococcus, Haemophilus, Streptococcus
